name: Rep. John Larson
tweet: What actions do I need to take now?   Other than filing your 2021 taxes to ensure the IRS has your up to date family information, no action is needed at this time. Keep an eye out for more information later this year on how the monthly delivery will work.

name: Rep. John Larson
tweet: How long will these payments be made for?   This credit is currently only for 2021, but I’m fighting to make them permanent!

name: Rep. John Larson
tweet: Who qualifies for this credit?   Individuals who make up to $75,000 a year or joint filers making up to $150,000 a year qualify for the tax credit. From there, the funds will be reduced by 50 dollars for every additional one thousand dollars of gross income.

name: Rep. John Larson
tweet: How will this credit be delivered?   For the first time, a portion of the credit will be delivered monthly, beginning in the second half of this year. Families will receive the rest when filing 2021 taxes in 2022.

name: Rep. John Larson
tweet: How much will qualifying families receive?   Families with children under 6 will receive an increased credit, totaling $3,600 per child. Families with children ages 6-17 will receive a total credit of $3K per child.   Family income can no longer be too low to receive the credit.

name: Rep. John Larson
tweet: The expansion of the #ChildTaxCredit championed by my friend @RosaDeLauro was included in the #AmericanRescuePlan and will cut childhood poverty across the country by 50%! That’s a pretty big deal. Here’s some FAQ we’ve received ⬇️

tweet: Today I’m voting to extend the bipartisan Paycheck Protection Program (PPP) that was set to expire at the end of March.   So many businesses in CT & across the nation have been able to stay afloat because of this federal program & I’m proud to vote to extend it until June 30th.

name: Rep. John Larson
tweet: For more information on stimulus checks visit  https://t.co/wMN2gjEjIe

name: Rep. John Larson
tweet: What if I don’t usually file taxes?  The IRS is urging people who don’t normally file a tax return to look into their filing options. Visit  https://t.co/vlh4eSlfQI for more information about options available.

name: Rep. John Larson
tweet: What if the IRS does not have my information?  You can input your direct deposit information using the IRS’s “Get My Payment” tool, see link below!  https://t.co/Pphn4NVtnk

name: Rep. John Larson
tweet: How will I get my payment?  Payments will be directly deposited into bank accounts, or come in the mail as a paper check or debit card. Visit the IRS’s "Get My Payment” tool to find out how the IRS will be delivering your payment.

name: Rep. John Larson
tweet: What do I need to do to claim my payment?  Most eligible people will get the 3rd stimulus check automatically & won’t need to take more action. The IRS will use available information from your most recent tax return to determine eligibility & which bank account to send it to.

name: Rep. John Larson
tweet: Do qualifying dependents also receive a check? What about adult dependents?  Yes and yes! Qualifying families receive $1,400 for each dependent. That includes adult dependents over the age of 17 too! So if you’re a single mother with 2 dependents, you will receive $4,200!

name: Rep. John Larson
tweet: Will I get a stimulus check if I make over 75K?  Maybe. Individuals making under $80,000 and married filers making under $160,000 will receive a reduced payment. Households over those incomes will not receive a payment.

name: Rep. John Larson

name: Rep. John Larson
tweet: The #AmericanRescuePlan would cut childhood poverty in half and provide critical COVID relief. Pass it on.

name: Rep. John Larson
tweet: I just voted to provide $1,400 in direct payments to Americans. I worked to ensure that the eligibility threshold wasn't lowered. Now isn't the time to risk those who need benefits losing out. I've heard from countless in CT about how vital these payments are to them during COVID

name: Rep. John Larson
tweet: The provision will also finally expand the EITC to include seniors! It's simply not right that you can owe more taxes, simply because you turned 65 and are getting closer to retirement. We're fixing that!

name: Rep. John Larson
tweet: I voted for a provision in the #AmericanRescuePlan to benefit 154,000 workers in CT. Expanding the Earned Income Tax Credit will help many low-income workers on the front lines of the pandemic. This will help servers, cashiers, home health aids & others!

name: Rep. John Larson
tweet: This expansion also raises thresholds for the Child Tax Credit to include middle class families. In Connecticut, this will help more than 720,000 children! 83% of children in CT will benefit from this! This wouldn't have been possible without the work of Chairwoman @RosaDeLauro!

name: Rep. John Larson
tweet: BIG NEWS: The expansion of the Child Tax Credit will cut childhood poverty in HALF! The #AmericanRescuePlan will increase the tax credit to $3600 for a child under 6 & $3000 for all other children. This is needed now more than ever due to the pandemic.

name: Rep. John Larson
tweet: I’m participating in the Ways&Means markup of COVID relief, but I’m also keeping an eye on the Senate #Impeachment trial. The House managers are making an overwhelming case that the attack on the Capitol occurred because of Pres. Trump’s lies & incitement. The Senate must convict

tweet: My Republican colleagues praise Operation Warp Speed and certainly moved at warp speed for a tax cut for the nation's 1%, when they forced it through Congress. Yet, they move at a tortoise speed to get relief for every day Americans. Our neighbors can’t wait any longer.

tweet: I voted to ensure those who are unemployed due to COVID can continue their health benefits and expand access to the ACA marketplace during the pandemic. This is an economic & health crisis- access to affordable, quality health coverage is critical! #AmericanRescuePlan

tweet: .@WaysMeansCmte Dems introduced a provision to lower the cost of health benefits for unemployed Americans. The GOP instead voted to cut off this critical assistance, leaving everyone unemployed in CT behind. @WaysMeansCmte Democrats blocked this cruel move!

tweet: Republicans just tried to use the pandemic to make an unprecedented attack on women's healthcare in private insurance. The COVID pandemic cannot be used to further a radical, anti-choice agenda, and we voted to stop them.
